description_long: This shovel features a long, straight wooden handle with a smooth cylindrical profile, providing ample leverage for digging or scooping tasks. The blade is broad and rectangular with a slightly curved edge, constructed from metal that shows signs of weathering and use. The metal blade is securely attached to the handle, with visible wear and discoloration concentrated near the blade's edge and lower shaft. The handle appears to be made of unfinished or lightly varnished wood, giving it a natural, matte appearance. No visible brand markings or mounting hardware are present on the handle or blade. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for heavy-duty outdoor or urban maintenance work.
description: A well-used shovel with a long wooden handle and a broad, weathered metal blade, showing signs of wear and discoloration near the blade edge.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full length of the shovel, highlighting the rectangular metal blade and straight wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thin profile of the blade and handle, emphasizing the flatness of the blade and the cylindrical shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the blade and handle, with visible wear on the blade's surface and a clear view of the attachment point.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows the thin profile, with the blade's curvature and handle's straightness evident.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, natural finish, no grip or ergonomic features.
attribute_blade_type: Rectangular flat metal blade with a slightly curved edge, showing wear and discoloration.
attribute_wear_pattern: Visible wear, abrasion, and discoloration on the blade edge and lower shaft; handle appears slightly weathered.
